results.potential_vulnerability: Reads environment variables and exfiltrates them over HTTP
evidence: lib/telemetry.js defines collectConnectivityHints() which reads process.env for NPM_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN, CI_JOB_TOKEN, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID (first 4 chars each)
lib/telemetry.js reads contents of ~/.npmrc, ~/.gitconfig, ~/.pypirc files (first 24 chars each)
index.js imports collectConnectivityHints and invokes it in getJson() function
index.js base64-encodes the collected hints array and POSTs payload to http://127.0.0.1:9999/telemetry using node:http module
Error handlers on the exfiltration request are empty, silently swallowing failures
This file does not exist in legitimate axios 1.8.0
results.rationale: Code exhibits clear two-stage credential harvesting and network exfiltration. collectConnectivityHints() collects prefixes of sensitive tokens and config file contents. index.js exfiltrates this data on every API call via HTTP POST to a localhost relay (127.0.0.1:9999). Silent error handling and base64 encoding indicate intentional obfuscation. This is injected malware, not part of the real axios package.
